SMW Credits Creator v1.1

The ultimate tool to automate RHR credits generation.

  • Quick Start
  • Character Mapping
  • Bulk Editor
  • Credits Data
  • Troubleshooting

🚀 Quick Start (Installation)

Portable: No installation required. Just unzip and run!
  1. Unzip the downloaded archive completely.
  2. Run SMWCreditsCreator.exe.
  3. Select Project: Click Select RHR Folder and choose your project root.
  4. Fix Config (If needed): If the tool warns you about configuration, click Fix Configuration to auto-repair.

🎨 Character Mapping Tab

This tab allows you to define exactly which global ExGFX tile corresponds to which text character. These settings are saved in mapping.json.

Delete Mapping: Made a mistake? Click the red ✖ button next to a character to clear it instantly.

Visual Graphics Preview

  • Loading Graphics: Click Load Graphics (.bin) to select your credits font file (e.g., ExGFX80.bin).
  • Palette:
    • Load Palette (.bin): Load a palette file.
    • Auto-Load: If a file named palette.pal exists in the tool's folder, it loads automatically on startup!
  • 4x Zoom: The preview is zoomed 400% so you can easily identify individual pixels and tiles.

Picking Tiles

You don't have to type Hex IDs manually!

  1. Click the red Target button 🎯 next to a character (e.g., 'A').
  2. Your cursor turns into a crosshair.
  3. Click the desired tile in the Graphics Preview image.
  4. The Hex ID is automatically inserted!
Advanced Picking: For 16x16 tiles, clicking a tile will automatically select a valid 16x16 block (Top-Left, Top-Right, etc.) based on standard logic.

Tile Sizes

Select your font size at the top: 8x8, 8x16, or 16x16. The grid overlay and picking logic will adjust automatically.

⚡ Bulk Editor

Use the Bulk Editor to map dozens of characters in seconds. This window is non-blocking, meaning you can keep it open while clicking around the main window to check tile numbers.

Reset: Check "Clear existing mappings first" to verify you start with a clean slate.

Syntax Guide

Enter one rule per line. Hex IDs are used for the starting tile.

Rule Type Syntax Example Description
Simple Range Start-End = ID A-Z = 280 Maps A->280, B->281, etc. sequentially.
Character Set [Chars] = ID [.,!?] = 300 Maps '.'->300, ','->301, '!'->302, '?'->303.
Single Char Char = ID @ = 1F0 Maps exactly one character.
Multi-Tile Char = ID1, ID2 A = 200, 202 Assigns multiple tiles to one character. (e.g. for 16x16 definition)
Comment # Text # This is a comment Lines starting with # are ignored.

Example Configuration

# Standard Font Mapping
A-Z = 280
a-z = 2A0
0-9 = 2C0

# Special Punctuation
[.,:;!?] = 2D0
# Map the heart symbol to tile 1F2
♥ = 1F2

📝 Credits Data

Go to the Credits Tab to input your actual credits text.

Input Methods

1. Online Generator (Recommended):
Go to saphros.de/smwcredits/ to visually organize your credits, then click "Download JSON" and load it into the tool.

2. Manual Text File:
Write a standard text file locally:

Header Name:
Person 1
Person 2

Another Header:
Person A
Person B

❓ Troubleshooting

Canvas stays black / No Preview?

Ensure you have loaded a valid 4BPP Graphics file (ExGFX). If it's still black, check if your Palette is loaded correctly. Try reloading the default palette.

"Callisto" errors?

The tool requires a valid RHR project structure. It looks for callisto.exe or the tools folder. Ensure you selected the root folder of your project.

Tile IDs are wrong in game?

Check the GFX Slot setting (BG2 vs BG3). BG2 starts at 0x200, BG3 starts at 0x280. Use the converter button ⇄ BG3 to swap all your current mappings if you mapped them for the wrong slot.

© 2026 Saphros | SMW Credits Creator v1.0.0
